evals.query: Create a UserService in my Kora app that depends on a UserRepository.
expected_behavior: Annotates the class with @Component from ru.tinkoff.kora.common
Declares the class final with a single public constructor that takes UserRepository (constructor injection)
Stores the dependency in a private final field
Does not use field injection, @Autowired-style annotations, or runtime reflection; uses only Kora @Component constructor injection

evals.query: Set up the @KoraApp entry point for a new Kora service that uses HOCON config and Logback logging.
expected_behavior: Declares an interface annotated with @KoraApp that extends HoconConfigModule and LogbackModule
Provides a static main that calls KoraApplication.run(ApplicationGraph::graph)
Imports KoraApplication from ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph
Explains that ApplicationGraph is generated at compile time in the same package and only one @KoraApp is allowed
Does not introduce a runtime DI container or a self-instantiated application class; relies on the compile-time generated ApplicationGraph

evals.query: I need to expose a HikariCP DataSource to my Kora container. How do I register it?
expected_behavior: Creates a @Module interface with a default factory method returning DataSource
Notes that a @Module in the same source set as @KoraApp is auto-discovered and needs no extends
Explains the factory method must not return null
Does not use @Bean, @Configuration, or @Provides

evals.query: I have two Cache implementations, RedisCache and CaffeineCache, both implementing Cache. How do I inject the Redis one specifically?
expected_behavior: Defines simple tag classes (for example RedisTag) and annotates implementations with @Tag(RedisTag.class)
Injects with @Tag(RedisTag.class) Cache at the constructor parameter
Explains that the same tag class must appear at both registration and injection point
Does not use @Qualifier or string-based qualifiers

evals.query: I want a NotificationService that fans out to every registered Notifier implementation in the container.
expected_behavior: Injects All<Notifier> from ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph in the constructor
Explains that All<T> extends List<T> and collects every component of that type
Optionally mentions @Tag(Tag.Any.class) All<Notifier> to also include tagged components
Does not rely on classpath scanning or reflection to discover implementations

evals.query: I'm writing a reusable Kora library module that provides a default ObjectMapper, but the application should be able to replace it.
expected_behavior: Marks the library factory method with @DefaultComponent from ru.tinkoff.kora.common
Shows the application overriding it with a plain factory method of the same type (no @DefaultComponent)
Explains that a non-default factory takes precedence over a @DefaultComponent one
Does not use @Primary or profile-based bean selection

evals.query: How do I break a circular dependency between ServiceA and ServiceB in Kora without restructuring the code?
expected_behavior: Wraps one side as ValueOf<ServiceB> in the constructor and accesses it via get() when needed
Explains that ValueOf is an indirect link so a refresh of the wrapped component does not cascade to the holder
Mentions that ValueOf exposes get() and refresh()
Does not use lazy proxies, setter injection, or reflection to break the cycle

evals.query: A component created by a @Module factory needs init and cleanup hooks. How do I give a factory-built component a lifecycle in Kora?
expected_behavior: Returns Wrapped<T> from the factory method and constructs new LifecycleWrapper<>(value, initCallback, releaseCallback)
Imports LifecycleWrapper and Wrapped from ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph
Mentions that components owned by a class can instead implement the Lifecycle interface with init() and release()
Notes release runs in reverse order of initialization on shutdown
Does not use @PostConstruct or @PreDestroy

evals.query: I have a CacheWarmer that must run at startup even though nothing depends on it. How do I force Kora to initialize it?
expected_behavior: Annotates the component (or factory method) with @Root so it is always initialized at startup
Explains that components are only created when they are a @Root or a transitive dependency of one
Imports @Root from ru.tinkoff.kora.common.annotation
Does not use an ApplicationRunner or a runtime startup hook

evals.query: We are splitting our Kora service into separate Gradle modules. How do I expose the components of a domain Gradle module to the application module?
expected_behavior: Marks the domain module's interface with @KoraSubmodule so Kora builds a module from its @Module and @Component types
Has the @KoraApp interface in the assembly module extends the submodule interface
Explains that each Gradle module needs the annotation-processors dependency to generate code
Does not assume modules from other Gradle projects are auto-discovered without extends
